---
name: ce-ask-deployment-verification-agent
description: "Produces Go/No-Go deployment checklists with SQL verification queries, rollback procedures, and monitoring plans. Use when PRs touch production data, migrations, or risky data changes."
---

<!-- Generated by ce-lite converter — do not edit. Regenerate via converter/generate_wrappers.py. -->

Dispatch the **ce-deployment-verification-agent** specialist as a sub-agent.

## Steps

1. Run `ce-lite-persona ce-deployment-verification-agent --body` via Bash. The resolver is on
   PATH (this plugin's `bin/` is exported by Claude Code) and prints the
   persona's full role prompt to stdout. On non-zero exit, the resolver's
   stderr explains the cause (unknown persona, missing prompt file, plugin
   not installed) — surface it and stop. Do not silently fall back to a
   different persona.

2. Spawn an `Agent` with `subagent_type: "general-purpose"` and
   `description: "ce-deployment-verification-agent: <one-line task summary>"` so traces stay
   readable. The prompt is the resolver output followed by this preamble
   and then the user-supplied task ($ARGUMENTS):

   ```
   [ce-persona=ce-deployment-verification-agent via=ce-ask-direct]

   You are operating as the ce-deployment-verification-agent specialist. Manifest declares
   this role uses tools=[Read, Grep, Glob, Bash] and model=inherit. Honour
   those constraints: if a task pulls you toward tools outside that set,
   stop and explain why your role requires it — don't silently broaden
   scope.
   ```

For a parallel multi-persona dispatch, see `/ce-ask-panel`.

Trace tag `[ce-persona=ce-deployment-verification-agent via=ce-ask-direct]` is inline debug
metadata; `grep` transcripts when investigating routing questions.
